evals.prompt: Check whether the docs are still accurate for what's in flight on this branch before I open a PR.
evals.expected_output: Detects that WorkspaceEvent gained a ScanCompleted variant on this branch while docs/consumer-guide.md's WorkspaceEvent enumeration does not mention it. Reports the divergence with file:line evidence on both sides and a proposed fix, without editing anything.
assertions: Detects that the branch adds a ScanCompleted variant to WorkspaceEvent and flags docs/consumer-guide.md's WorkspaceEvent enumeration as missing it
Cites file:line evidence on both sides (the doc claim and the code location) for the divergence
Proposes a concrete fix (a doc bullet for ScanCompleted) rather than only naming the file
Does not edit any files in the worktree
Does not flag point-in-time artifacts (brainstorms, plans, ADRs, architecture.html) as stale

evals.prompt: Are the docs still in sync with the changes on this branch?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the branch only adds unit tests and reports a clean verdict with a 'checked and current' account, without inventing spurious findings or editing docs.
assertions: Reaches a clean verdict (no divergences) for the test-only branch
Explicitly reasons that the change is test-only / non-public-API rather than just asserting cleanliness
Includes a 'checked and current' style account of which doc surfaces were verified
Does not invent spurious findings (e.g. demanding docs for private test helpers)
Does not edit any files in the worktree

evals.prompt: Did anything go stale docs-wise with my current changes? I haven't committed yet.
evals.expected_output: Catches the uncommitted MAX_FILE_SIZE change (1 MiB -> 4 MiB) and reports the stale '1 MiB' claims in filter.rs module rustdoc and docs/roadmap.md.
assertions: Includes the uncommitted working-tree change (MAX_FILE_SIZE 1 MiB -> 4 MiB) in the audit
Flags the stale module-level rustdoc in crates/artel-fs/src/filter.rs that still says '1 MiB'
Flags docs/roadmap.md's '1 MiB' mention as stale
Cites file:line evidence on both sides for each divergence
Does not edit any files in the worktree
